column4: YOU NOTICE A CONFIGURATION CHANGE YOUR TEAMMATE MADE LAST WEEK MAY BE AFFECTING SYSTEM PERFORMANCE. NO INCIDENTS HAVE BEEN REPORTED YET.
column6: Assume monitoring tools will catch serious problems
column7: Flag it to your team and investigate the potential impact immediately

column4: A TEAMMATE URGENTLY NEEDS ACCESS TO COMPLETE A TASK AND ASKS TO BORROW YOUR PASSWORD TEMPORARILY.
column6: Share it and change the password later
column7: Decline and direct them to request proper access

column4: YOU REALISE YOU STILL HAVE PRODUCTION ACCESS FROM A PREVIOUS PROJECT, EVEN THOUGH YOU NO LONGER NEED IT.
column6: Request for the access to be removed
column7: Ignore it since you are not actively using it

column4: A TEAMMATE SUGGESTS TEMPORARILY BYPASSING A MONITORING ALERT BECAUSE IT HAS BEEN "TRIGGERING TOO OFTEN."
column6: Investigate why the alert is triggering before making changes
column7: Turn it off and rely on manual checks

column4: DURING DEPLOYMENT, A TEAMMATE SUGGESTS MAKING A QUICK PRODUCTION TWEAK OUTSIDE THE APPROVED CHANGE REQUEST SCOPE.
column6: Make the change and update documentation later
column7: Reject the change and follow the approved process

column4: A TEAM WANTS TO DEPLOY MANUALLY BECAUSE THE CICD PIPELINE IS TEMPORARILY SLOW.
column6: Use CICD unless a formal exemption exists
column7: Approve manual deployment to avoid delays

column4: A SECURITY SCAN IDENTIFIES VULNERABILITIES THAT THE TEAM BELIEVES ARE "LOW RISK."
column6: Ensure findings are assessed and remediated as required

column4: YOU NOTICE RELEASE DOCUMENTATION DOES NOT CLEARLY MAP REQUIREMENTS TO CODE CHANGES AND TEST CASES.
column7: Ensure proper traceability is completed before deployment

column4: A PRODUCTION ISSUE OCCURS AFTER A DEPLOYMENT INVOLVING MULTIPLE TEAMS. YOUR TEAM'S COMPONENT MAY HAVE CONTRIBUTED, BUT ROOT CAUSE IS NOT CONFIRMED.
column6: Proactively raise potential impact and support investigation efforts

column4: A DEVELOPER PROPOSES REMOVING AN OLD VALIDATION STEP BECAUSE IT APPEARS REDUNDANT AND SLOWS PERFORMANCE.
column6: Assess whether it acts as a hidden safeguard elsewhere in the system
column7: Remove it temporarily and monitor production

column4: YOU NOTICE EXPERIENCED TEAM MEMBERS REGULARLY BYPASS SMALL PROCESS STEPS BECAUSE "NOTHING HAS GONE WRONG BEFORE."
column7: Raise concerns and continue following established processes

column4: A CRITICAL CUSTOMER ISSUE REQUIRES URGENT INTERVENTION, BUT PROPER ACCESS HAS NOT YET BEEN GRANTED.
column6: Suppress alerts until patterns become clearer
column7: Escalate and investigate the abnormal behaviour
